nativeRange.habitat: Shallow intertidal and subtidal rocky and reef substrates in warm tropical seas

decorPreferences: anchored to rockwork in moderate flow

avoidWith: aggressive herbivorous fish that may heavily graze it

Additional product photo from Mosaic Macros listing.

summary: A rare, slow-growing red macroalga with fine filaments covering its branches for a distinctively fuzzy appearance. Seldom seen in the hobby; best suited to mature display reef tanks.
careNotes:
Digenea simplex (family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) is a bushy red macroalga whose branches are densely covered with fine filaments, producing the characteristic fuzzy or antler-like appearance that gives it its trade name. It is widely distributed across tropical and subtropical seas — the Mediterranean, tropical Atlantic, Caribbean, and Indo-Pacific — where it grows attached to hard substrates in the shallow photic zone.

This species is historically notable as the natural source of kainic acid, a compound traditionally used as an anthelmintic (anti-parasitic) in East Asian herbal medicine, and later studied extensively in neuroscience.

In the aquarium hobby, Digenea simplex is rarely available. Mosaic Macros is believed to be the sole current hobby vendor. The Mosaic product page notes very slow growth and limited availability. No detailed aquarium husbandry data was located from hobby sources; the water parameters, lighting, and other values in this entry are extrapolated from standard reef conditions and family-level (Rhodomelaceae) care norms for ornamental red macroalgae.

Growth form is upright and bushy, generally anchored to rockwork. Medium lighting is generally advised for ornamental Rhodomelaceae; high flow tends to be beneficial for gas exchange. Propagation is typically by simple fragmentation in captivity. Nutrient uptake is considered low relative to refugium macros such as Chaetomorpha. No allelopathic concerns have been documented for this genus.

DATA STATUS NOTE: Only one hobby source (Mosaic Macros) was found; no parameter data was published on that page. All numeric and husbandry fields are extrapolated from family-level norms and standard reef parameters. Reviewer should confirm or correct with additional sources if available.
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is typically by fragmentation. The life cycle in the wild involves alternation of generations between gametophyte and tetrasporophyte phases (as in most Rhodomelaceae), but this has not been documented as a meaningful propagation route in the hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Product page confirms scientific name Digenea simplex, bushy form with fine filaments, very slow growth, and rarity in the hobby. No numeric water parameters provided.

additional.name: NCBI Taxonomy
additional.notes: Confirms taxonomy: Kingdom Eukaryota, Phylum Rhodophyta, Class Florideophyceae, Order Ceramiales, Family Rhodomelaceae. Basionym Conferva simplex Wulfen 1803, reclassified by C. Agardh 1822.

additional.name: Macroalgae Herbarium Portal
additional.notes: Confirms Family Rhodomelaceae. Notes synonyms including Caulerpa lycopodium, Ceramium digenea, Conferva simplex, Digenea lycopodium. Herbarium specimens from broad geographic range confirming wide tropical/subtropical distribution.
